factors.summary: CapEx is stable with no significant changes indicated.

evidence.text: The Company has not utilized third party evidence of selling price.
evidence.subsection: Revenue Recognition, Deferred Revenue and Accounts and Notes Receivable

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: CapEx is stable with no significant changes indicated.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is decreasing.

evidence.text: Net cash used in financing activities was $33.8 million during the first nine months of 2016.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow is decreasing, which may indicate financial strain or reduced profitability.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are increasing, driven by anticipation of higher future sales.

evidence.text: Cash from operations also decreased due to increases in accounts and notes receivable of $5.3 million driven by higher sales and increases in inventory of $10.8 million as the Company continues to invest in anticipation of higher future sales.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Inventory levels are increasing due to anticipation of higher future sales, suggesting positive demand expectations.

factors.summary: Product quality is stable with no significant issues mentioned.

evidence.text: The Company warranties its CEWs, Axon cameras and E-Docks from manufacturing defects on a limited basis for a period of one year after purchase and, thereafter, will replace any defective unit for a fee.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Product quality is stable with no significant issues mentioned.

factors.summary: Order backlog is growing, with new orders outpacing revenue.

evidence.text: Bookings related to Evidence.com and Axon products and services, net of cancellations, were $57.5 million and $36.9 million during the three months ended September 30, 2016 and 2015 , respectively, an increase of $20.6 million , or 55.9% .

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Order backlog is growing, indicating increased demand and potential future revenue growth.

factors.summary: The Axon segment is growing fastest, driven by increasing adoption of on-officer cameras and digital evidence management software.

evidence.text: Net sales for the Axon segment were $18.9 million and $10.9 million for the three months ended September 30, 2016 and 2015 , respectively, an increase of $8.1 million or 74.5% . The overall increase in the Axon segment was driven by continued adoption of on-officer cameras and related technologies, including the Company's Evidence.com digital evidence management software suite.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The Axon segment is growing rapidly, driven by increasing adoption of key products and software, indicating strong market momentum.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, driven by growth in the company's workforce and related expenses.

evidence.text: Sales, general and administrative (“SG&A”) expenses were comprised as follows for the three months ended September 30, 2016 and 2015 (dollars in thousands): [...] Salaries, benefits and bonus $ 10,956   $ 6,402   $ 4,554   71.1 % [...] SG&A expenses were $28.1 million and $17.8 million for the three months ended September 30, 2016 and 2015 , respectively, an increase of $10.3 million or 57.7% .

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing due to workforce growth and related expenses, which could negatively impact margins.

factors.summary: Management is confident, as evidenced by increased investments in R&D and scaling of the Axon segment.

evidence.text: The Company remains focused on growing the Axon segment as it adds headcount and external resources to develop new products and services to further advance its scalable cloud-connected device platform. These increases were partially offset by decreases in professional and consulting of $0.3 million related primarily to the Company being more selective in the utilization of consultants versus hiring additional internal resources.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management is confident, as shown by increased investments in R&D and scaling of the Axon segment, indicating a positive outlook.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include potential budgetary constraints and political factors affecting government contracts, as well as uncertainties related to Brexit.

evidence.text: Such factors include, but are not limited to: the adverse effect of the United Kingdom's exit from the European Union; market acceptance of our products; [...] budgetary and political constraints of prospects and customers; [...] risks of governmental regulations, including regulations of our products by the United States Consumer Product Safety Commission, [...] and foreign regulation and the adverse effects that could result from our products being classified as firearms by the United States Bureau of Alcohol and Firearms; [...] and other factors identified in documents filed by us with the Securities and Exchange Commission, including those set forth in our Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2015 .
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Item 7. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include potential budgetary constraints and political factors affecting government contracts, as well as uncertainties related to Brexit.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy is clear, focusing on expanding its Axon segment and advancing its digital evidence management solutions.

evidence.text: TASER International, Inc.’s (the “Company” or “TASER” or “we” or “our”) core mission is to protect life and to protect truth through technologies that make communities safer. We are the market leader in the development, manufacture and sale of conducted electrical weapons (“CEWs”) and other electronic weapons designed for use in law enforcement, military, corrections, private security and personal defense. To address challenges faced by law enforcement officers subsequent to post-incident, we have developed a fully integrated hardware and software solution to provide our law enforcement customers the capabilities to capture, store, manage, share and analyze video and other digital evidence.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company's long-term strategy is clear, focusing on expanding its Axon segment and advancing its digital evidence management solutions.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include government budgetary constraints, regulatory changes, and foreign currency fluctuations.

evidence.text: Such factors include, but are not limited to: [...] budgetary and political constraints of prospects and customers; [...] risks of governmental regulations, including regulations of our products by the United States Consumer Product Safety Commission, regulation of our products as a 'crime control' product by the Federal government, state and local government regulation and foreign regulation and the adverse effects that could result from our products being classified as firearms by the United States Bureau of Alcohol and Firearms; [...] and other factors identified in documents filed by us with the Securities and Exchange Commission, including those set forth in our Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2015 .
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Item 7. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Key sensitivities include government budgetary constraints, regulatory changes, and foreign currency fluctuations.

factors.summary: A key catalyst is the continued adoption of the company's Axon platform, driven by new contracts and increased active users.

evidence.text: The Company is continuing to enter into new contracts with domestic and international agencies seeking to deploy its technologies. Evidence.com revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2016 increased $5.8 million to $8.5 million as compared to the same period in 2015 . This increase was primarily driven by an increase in active users on the Company's Axon platform, but was also due to catch up revenue adjustments for arrangements where services were being provided as contracted, but certain revenue recognition criteria precluded the Company from recognizing the service revenues earlier.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: A key catalyst is the continued adoption of the company's Axon platform, driven by new contracts and increased active users.

factors.summary: Regulatory changes, particularly related to government contracts and product classifications, pose risks but also present opportunities.

evidence.text: Such factors include, but are not limited to: [...] risks of governmental regulations, including regulations of our products by the United States Consumer Product Safety Commission, regulation of our products as a 'crime control' product by the Federal government, state and local government regulation and foreign regulation and the adverse effects that could result from our products being classified as firearms by the United States Bureau of Alcohol and Firearms; [...] and other factors identified in documents filed by us with the Securities and Exchange Commission, including those set forth in our Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2015 .
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Item 7. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Regulatory changes pose risks but also present opportunities, indicating a mixed impact.

factors.summary: New products, particularly in the Axon segment, are gaining traction with customers.

evidence.text: The overall increase in the Axon segment was driven by continued adoption of on-officer cameras and related technologies, including the Company's Evidence.com digital evidence management software suite. Recognized revenue related to the Company's on-officer cameras and related accessories increased approximately $2.7 million . The Company is continuing to enter into new contracts with domestic and international agencies seeking to deploy its technologies.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: New products, particularly in the Axon segment, are gaining traction with customers.

factors.summary: The company exhibits strong pricing power, as evidenced by stable gross margins and increased revenue without significant price changes. Additionally, the company is not forced to discount, further indicating maintained pricing power.

evidence.text: These non-cash impacts were partially offset by deferred income tax expense of $2.7 million.
As a percentage of net sales, gross margin increased slightly to 64.9% for the nine months ended September 30, 2016 compared to 64.6% for the nine months ended September 30, 2015.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company exhibits strong pricing power, as evidenced by stable gross margins and increased revenue without significant price changes.

factors.summary: The company faces significant litigation exposure, including product-related lawsuits and ongoing patent litigation. While it has accrued estimated loss contingencies, there remains uncertainty regarding the ultimate resolution of these matters, which could impact financial performance.

evidence.text: We are subject to the possibility of various loss contingencies arising in the ordinary course of business, including product-related litigation, . We consider the likelihood of loss or impairment of an asset or the incurrence of a liability, as well as our ability to reasonably estimate the amount of loss in determining loss contingencies. An estimated loss contingency is accrued when it is probable that an asset has been impaired or a liability has been incurred and the amount of loss can be reasonably estimated.
Included in the increase in professional expenses were legal fees related to the on-going patent litigation suit filed by a competitor of the Company.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company faces significant litigation exposure, including product-related lawsuits and ongoing patent litigation, with uncertainty regarding financial impact.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing across multiple product lines, driven by higher sales and strong demand, particularly in cartridge sales and the Axon segment.

evidence.text: Cash from operations also decreased due to increases in accounts and notes receivable of $5.3 million driven by higher sales and increases in inventory of $10.8 million as the Company continues to invest in anticipation of higher future sales.
Net unit sales for cartridges increased $235,538 or 19.8%. Net unit sales for the Axon segment increased significantly, with Axon Body units growing 166.4% and Axon Flex units growing 35.3%.

Net unit sales for the TASER Weapons handles and other products and Axon segment products are as follows: [...] TASER X26P 23,259   13,659   9,600   70.3  % [...] Axon Flex 4,961   6,759   (1,798 )   (26.6 ) [...] Axon Body 25,093   4,778   20,315   425.2  %

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing across multiple product lines, driven by higher sales and strong demand.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, with both the TASER Weapons and Axon segments showing significant increases. Strong performance across both product segments contributed to this growth, as evidenced by substantial year-over-year increases in net sales.

evidence.text: Net sales were $186.2 million and $141.9 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2016 and 2015, respectively, an increase of $44.3 million or 31.2%. Net sales for the TASER Weapons segment were $144.3 million and $115.7 million for nine months ended September 30, 2016 and 2015, respectively, an increase of $28.6 million or 24.7%. Net sales for the Axon segment were $41.9 million and $26.2 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2016 and 2015, respectively, an increase of $15.7 million or 60.0%.
Net sales were $71.9 million and $50.4 million for the three months ended September 30, 2016 and 2015 , respectively, an increase of $21.5 million or 42.7% .

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Both the TASER Weapons and Axon segments show significant organic revenue increases, indicating strong performance.

factors.summary: The company's margins show a mixed trajectory. While gross margins have been expanding, operating margins are under pressure due to increased SG&A expenses, particularly in sales and marketing.

evidence.text: Gross margin as a percentage of net sales increased slightly to 64.9%. However, SG&A expenses as a percentage of net sales increased to 41.5% from 33.7%, impacting operating margins.
evidence.subsection: Revenue Recognition, Deferred Revenue and Accounts and Notes Receivable

evidence.text: Gross margin increased $15.5 million to $46.6 million for the three months ended September 30, 2016 compared to $31.1 million for 2015. As a percentage of net sales, gross margin increased to 64.8% for the three months ended September 30, 2016 compared to 61.7% for 2015.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: While gross margins are expanding, operating margins are under pressure due to increased SG&A expenses, resulting in mixed signals.

factors.summary: Input costs have remained stable across the examined periods, with no significant changes reported. While there is a slight increase in variable manufacturing costs within the TASER Weapons segment, this increase is offset by leveraging of fixed overhead costs and does not indicate a substantial shift in overall input cost trends.

evidence.text: The allocation of manufacturing labor and overhead costs includes management’s judgments of what constitutes normal capacity of our production facilities and a determination of what costs are considered to be abnormal fixed production costs, which are expensed as current period charges.

Within the TASER Weapons segment, cost of products sold increased to $15.0 million for the three months ended September 30, 2016 from $12.4 million in 2015 , but decreased as a percentage of sales to 28.3% from 31.5% , respectively. The decrease as a percentage of sales in the third quarter of 2016 compared to the same period in 2015 was primarily attributable to increased leveraging of fixed overhead costs partially offset by increased discounting. Variable manufacturing costs remained relatively consistent during these periods.
evidence.subsection: Cost of Products Sold and Services Delivered

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Input costs have remained stable with slight increases offset by leveraging of fixed overhead costs, indicating no significant shift in cost trends.

factors.summary: Demand trends are positive, with acceleration observed in the Axon segment driven by increased sales and customer adoption, as well as significant growth in bookings for Axon products and services.

evidence.text: The overall increase in the Axon segment was driven by continued adoption of on-officer cameras and related technology. The Company continued to see increased recurring revenues related to its long-term Evidence.com arrangements.

Bookings related to Evidence.com and Axon products and services, net of cancellations, were $57.5 million and $36.9 million during the three months ended September 30, 2016 and 2015 , respectively, an increase of $20.6 million , or 55.9% .

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Positive demand trends with acceleration in the Axon segment driven by increased sales and customer adoption.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly the Axon segment, which has seen a significant increase in gross margin. This shift is contributing to an overall improvement in the company's gross margins, as evidenced by the increase from 61.7% to 64.8% in the period under review.

evidence.text: Gross margin for the Axon segment increased to 48.9% from 41.2%, indicating a shift towards higher-margin products. The TASER Weapons segment maintained a stable gross margin.

Gross margin increased $15.5 million to $46.6 million for the three months ended September 30, 2016 compared to $31.1 million for 2015. As a percentage of net sales, gross margin increased to 64.8% for the three months ended September 30, 2016 compared to 61.7% for 2015.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly Axon, contributing to improved gross margins.

factors.summary: The company is increasing its R&D investment, particularly in the Axon segment, to support new product development and innovation. The increase is driven by higher salaries and benefits, including stock-based compensation, in the Axon segment.

evidence.text: R&D expenses were $21.0 million and $21.0 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2016 and 2015, respectively, an increase of $4.0 million or 23.6%. The increase is driven by salaries and benefits, inclusive of stock-based compensation, in the Axon segment.
R&D expenses were $7.4 million and $6.5 million for the three months ended September 30, 2016 and 2015, respectively, an increase of $0.8 million, or 12.7%.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing R&D investment supports new product development and innovation, particularly in the Axon segment.
